findings.evidence: `PointsFunctor.lean:35` has `variable (R H : Type u) [CommRing R] [Ring H]`; `FunctorOfPoints.lean:80` proves the Hopf group under `[Semiring H]`; `CommAlgCat/Basic.lean:24-35` defines `R : Type u` and `carrier : Type v`.
findings.fix: Use separate universes, e.g. `R : Type u`, `H : Type v`, category objects `Type w`, and replace `[Ring H]` by `[Semiring H]` where the Hopf group instance is used.
findings.issue: `AlgPoints` and `pointsFunctor` require `[Ring H]` and put `R`, `H`, and all value algebras in the same universe, although the packaged group instance only needs `[Semiring H]` and Mathlib's `CommAlgCat` separates the base and object universes.

findings.evidence: `PointsFunctor.lean:48-58` assumes `[_root_.HopfAlgebra R H]` for `map`; `FunctorOfPoints.lean:147-156` defines `AlgHom.mapValue` under `[_root_.Bialgebra R H]` and `[CommSemiring A]`.
findings.fix: Define the `AlgPoints` type and `AlgPoints.map`/simp lemmas under `[CommSemiring R] [Semiring H] [_root_.Bialgebra R H] [CommSemiring A] [CommSemiring B]`, then add the `GrpCat` functor only in the Hopf/commutative-ring specialization required by `CommAlgCat`.
findings.issue: `AlgPoints.map` is specialized to Hopf algebras, but post-composition is already available for bialgebras and does not use the antipode.

summary: The new categorical wrapper is narrower than the convolution API it packages. Generalize the public point and map wrappers to the existing semiring/bialgebra level, and avoid tying all objects to one universe.
